title: PwC opens Shanghai AI Innovation Centre to drive AI-led value creation
excerpt: The centre draws on PwC’s global network, linking domestic innovation with international best practices.
content_html:
Ellichipuram Umesh

Thu, July 23, 2026 at 10:20 AM CDT 2 min read

PwC China has opened its AI Innovation Centre in Shanghai, positioning the new facility as a "key engine" of AI-led value creation.

The centre brings together internal research and development capabilities as well as broad-based industry support to create AI solutions designed for local market demand.

It draws on PwC's global network, linking domestic innovation with international best practices.

The aim is to help companies across sectors capture AI opportunities and generate measurable commercial value, the company said in a statement on 22 July.

More than 100 attendees took part in the official launch event.

They included representatives from the government, technology enterprises, ecosystem partners, academic institutions and industry associations, alongside PwC China's management team.

At the event, panel discussions followed the inauguration, with PwC China releasing its 2026 Intelligent Robotic white paper.

The discussions also brought together industry experts to examine future trends and implementation pathways for China's AI sector, while highlighting findings from the company's AI Performance Study.

PwC said the centre is built around five core pillars within its AI ecosystem strategy in China: research, factory, alliances & ecosystem, industrial exchange and talent development.

According to the company, these pillars span the full value chain, from advanced research through to real-world industry deployment.

It added that the centre's exhibition space is intended to function as a collaborative base for PwC specialists and clients, providing interactive demonstrations and joint prototyping to speed up the development of customised AI road maps.

The company also outlined plans to support businesses in launching their own AI transformation programmes efficiently.

PwC global chief AI officer Joe Atkinson said: "The Centre brings together PwC's global AI expertise with the strength of our local capabilities, positioning us to help both domestic enterprises and multinational clients operating in China adopt AI responsibly and effectively."

"PwC opens Shanghai AI Innovation Centre to drive AI-led value creation" was originally created and published by International Accounting Bulletin, a GlobalData owned brand.

content_html_zh:
埃利奇普拉姆·乌梅什

2026 年 7 月 23 日星期四上午 10:20 CDT 阅读 2 分钟

普华永道中国在上海开设人工智能创新中心，将新设施定位为人工智能主导价值创造的“关键引擎”。

该中心汇集了内部研发能力以及广泛的行业支持，以创建专为当地市场需求而设计的人工智能解决方案。

它利用普华永道的全球网络，将国内创新与国际最佳实践联系起来。

该公司在 7 月 22 日的一份声明中表示，此举的目的是帮助各行业的公司抓住人工智能机会并产生可衡量的商业价值。

超过 100 名与会者参加了官方发布会。

他们包括来自政府、科技企业、生态系统合作伙伴、学术机构和行业协会的代表，以及普华永道中国的管理团队。

发布会上，揭牌仪式结束后进行了圆桌讨论，普华永道中国发布了《2026智能机器人》白皮书。

讨论还汇集了行业专家，探讨中国人工智能领域的未来趋势和实施路径，同时重点介绍该公司人工智能绩效研究的结果。

普华永道表示，该中心围绕其中国人工智能生态系统战略的五个核心支柱建立：研究、工厂、联盟和生态系统、产业交流和人才发展。

据该公司称，这些支柱跨越了从高级研究到实际行业部署的整个价值链。

它补充说，该中心的展览空间旨在作为普华永道专家和客户的协作基地，提供互动演示和联合原型设计，以加快定制人工智能路线图的开发。

该公司还概述了支持企业有效启动自己的人工智能转型计划的计划。

普华永道全球首席人工智能官 Joe Atkinson 表示：“该中心将普华永道的全球人工智能专业知识与我们的本地能力优势相结合，帮助我们在中国运营的国内企业和跨国客户负责任且有效地采用人工智能。”

《普华永道上海人工智能创新中心成立，推动人工智能主导的价值创造》最初由 GlobalData 旗下品牌《国际会计公报》创作并发布。
